generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 robot, converting electrical current (I_M) to torque (τ_M) with manufacturer-provided torque conversion parameters."
]

instance GearWheel : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical gearwheel component in the first axis transmission system that transmits and amplifies torque (τ_G) from the motor to the cycloidal gearbox."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision cycloidal gearbox in the first axis transmission system that amplifies torque (τ_out) while reducing angular velocity, operating within the restricted working range [-90°, 90°]."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor measuring angular displacement (r_M) for motor angle feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionData
]

instance SecondaryPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind the cycloidal gearbox measuring angular displacement (r_G) of the gearwheel output shaft for backlash compensation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryPositionData
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring input current (I_M) to the permanent magnet synchronous motor for torque derivation using manufacturer-provided motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData
]

instance Sinumerik840DslController : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller providing time-series data for position, velocity, and acceleration via MQTT streaming."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ControllerTimeSeriesData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance SecondaryPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryPositionEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance ControllerTimeSeriesData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Sinumerik840DslController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance MotorAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured motor angle (r_M) data transmitted from MotorPositionEncoder for friction analysis and simulation input."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionData
]

instance GearWheelAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured gearwheel angle (r_G) data transmitted from SecondaryPositionEncoder for backlash compensation and friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryPositionData
]

instance MotorCurrentDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed motor current (I_M) data converted to torque (τ_M) using manufacturer-provided motor constants for dynamics simulations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData
]

instance ControllerPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series position data from SINUMERIK 840D sl controller including position, velocity, and acceleration for comparison with simulated outputs."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ControllerTimeSeriesData
]

instance BaseGearboxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Ideal gearbox dynamics model simulating torque transmission (τ_G → τ_out) without friction effects, serving as baseline for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulink
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical Coulomb-Viscous friction model implemented in Simulink for real-time friction compensation, incorporating static and viscous friction coefficients."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulink
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Stribeck friction model accounting for stiction and breakaway effects, implemented in Simulink for high-precision friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulink
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dahl friction model simulating hysteresis effects in gearbox friction, implemented in Simulink for dynamic friction characterization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulink
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "LuGre friction model capturing micro-sliding effects in gearbox joints, implemented in Simulink for advanced friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulink
]

instance TorqueDerivationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data-driven torque derivation model converting measured current (I_M) to torque (τ_M) using manufacturer-provided motor constants and real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulink
]

instance GearboxDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable gearbox dynamics model simulating torque transmission (τ_G → τ_out) with integrated friction models, implemented in Simulink."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulink
]

instance MotorDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable motor dynamics model simulating electrical-mechanical conversion (I_M → τ_M, ω_M) for friction analysis, implemented in Simulink."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulink
]

instance FrictionParameterUpdateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Real-time friction parameter identification model continuously updating static, kinetic, and viscous friction coefficients using measured vs. simulated deviations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulink
]

instance DeviationAnalysisModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Quantitative deviation analysis model comparing simulated (δr_out) vs. measured (r_out) outputs to highlight friction-induced inaccuracies in robot motion."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OperationalInsightDashboard
]

instance FrictionModelBase : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Base friction model framework providing common interface for all friction models in the system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulink
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML (Systems Modeling Language) tool used for structural modeling of the first axis components (BDDs and IBDs) to describe motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox interconnections."
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralModelingService
]

instance MathWorksSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink platform implementing executable simulation models for friction dynamics (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) and gearbox behavior."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelComparisonService, GearboxSimulationService
]

instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol enabling real-time streaming of time-series data (I_M, r_M, r_G) from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller to the digital shadow for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamingService
]

instance DigitalShadowReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM) providing concrete realization of the digital shadow with classes for Purpose (FrictionDerivation), DataTraces, Sources, and Processing components."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionDerivationService
]

instance OperationalInsightDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Visualization dashboard displaying measured vs. simulated data traces (torque, angular velocity) to enable semantic analysis of friction-induced inaccuracies."
    DTDFVocab:enables OperationalInsightService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionDerivationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous identification of joint friction parameters (static, kinetic, viscous) using white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models for real-time model refinement."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionParameterUpdateInsight
]

instance FrictionModelComparisonService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Comparison of white-box (Coulomb-Viscous, Dahl) vs. black-box (data-driven) friction models to inform model complexity trade-offs for improved accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelSelectionGuidanceInsight
]

instance DeviationAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Quantification of simulated vs. measured deviations (δr_out vs. r_out) to highlight friction-induced inaccuracies, guiding maintenance schedules or tooling adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviationAnalysisInsight
]

instance GearboxSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executable simulation of gearbox dynamics with integrated friction models to analyze torque transmission and angular velocity effects under load."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalInsightService
]

instance StructuralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "SysML-based structural modeling of the first axis components (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox) and their interconnections for digital twin foundation."
    DTDFVocab:provides AssetAnalysisInsight
]

instance AssetAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Analysis of the first axis as the scope of the digital shadow, modeling its structure and behavior for friction analysis purposes."
    DTDFVocab:provides AssetAnalysisInsight
]

instance DataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time streaming of sensor data (I_M, r_M, r_G) from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller to the digital shadow via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceabilityInsight
]

instance PhysicalToVirtualInteraction : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "One-way data flow from physical twin (PT) to digital shadow (DS) adhering to the Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM) for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceabilityInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionParameterUpdateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous identification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction coefficients enabling real-time model refinement for improved control accuracy."
]

instance ModelSelectionGuidanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comparison of white-box vs. black-box friction models informing decisions on model complexity trade-offs (parameter tuning vs. data-driven accuracy)."
]

instance DeviationAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantification of simulated vs. measured deviations (δr_out vs. r_out) highlighting friction-induced inaccuracies in robot motion."
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Guidance for maintenance schedules or tooling adjustments based on friction-induced inaccuracies detected in the deviation analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance OperationalInsightService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Visualization of torque and angular velocity traces to understand load-dependent friction effects, supporting process optimization (e.g., machining strategies)."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalInsightInsight
]

instance OperationalInsightInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualized torque and angular velocity traces enabling understanding of load-dependent friction effects for process optimization."
]

instance TorqueAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of torque transmission through the gearbox system to understand friction effects on system performance."
]

instance AssetAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural and behavioral analysis of the first axis components (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox) for digital twin foundation."
]

instance DataTraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "End-to-end traceability of data generation and computation linking data points to their sources (sensors, models, processing components)."
]

instance DeviationVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dashboard displaying measured vs. simulated data traces (torque, angular velocity) for semantic analysis of friction-induced deviations."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviationVisualizationInsight
]

instance DeviationVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of simulated vs. measured deviations enabling semantic understanding of friction-induced inaccuracies."
]

// Added missing environment and components
instance FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Environment containing the first axis components of MABI MAX 100 robot including motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearWheel, CycloidalGearbox, MotorPositionEncoder, SecondaryPositionEncoder, MotorCurrentSensor, Sinumerik840DslController
]

instance MabiMax100System : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "MABI MAX 100 industrial robot system under study for friction analysis in the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment FirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates on **real-time and near-real-time scales**:
  - **Data acquisition**: Sensors stream data at **high-frequency intervals** (exact frequency not specified but implied to be sufficient for capturing dynamic friction effects during robot operation).
  - **Model computation**: Simulation models (e.g., Simulink) process data traces **continuously during operation** to update friction parameters and detect deviations.
  - **Visualization updates**: The dashboard provides **real-time or near-real-time updates** of friction analysis results, enabling immediate insights into joint behavior.
  - **Synchronization**: The DS synchronizes with the PT’s operational timeline, ensuring alignment between **measured data (from PT)** and **simulated data (from DS)** for friction analysis."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is organized as follows:
  - **Physical Twin (PT)**: The **MABI MAX 100 first axis** (motor + gearboxes) with embedded sensors (encoders, current sensor) and CNC controller (SINUMERIK 840D sl).
  - **Digital Shadow (DS)**: A **real-time, data-driven representation** of the PT’s joint friction behavior, incorporating:
    - **Data Sources**: Sensors (MQTT-streamed data) and **derived data** (torque, velocity calculations).
    - **Processing Components**: Simulation models (Simulink) for friction analysis, linked to data traces via the DSRM.
    - **Outputs**: Visualized dashboard for friction analysis and **traceable data traces** (e.g., ˆr_M, ˆr_G) generated by processing components.
  - **Orchestration**: The DS is **purpose-driven**, designed to **continuously improve friction models** during operation. It does not include **autonomous control loops** or **feedback to the PT** but serves as a **baseline for future digital twin extensions** (e.g., closed-loop friction compensation)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates primarily during the **operational phase** of the robot’s life cycle, focusing on **as-operated** representation of the first axis. It does not model the **as-designed** (ideal) or **as-manufactured** phases explicitly but instead **continuously refines** its friction models based on real-time operational data. The DS is designed to **support ongoing maintenance and control optimization** by analyzing friction-induced inaccuracies during active use."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The digital twin’s fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model Verification**:
    - **Simulation models** (Simulink) are validated against **measured data traces** (e.g., comparing ˆr_M vs. r_M) to detect deviations caused by friction.
    - **Friction models** (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) are parameterized using **manufacturer-provided motor constants** and **real-time sensor data**.
  - **Uncertainty Handling**:
    - **Encoder comparisons** (motor vs. secondary encoder) reduce backlash and positional uncertainty.
    - **Data trace traceability**: Each data point is linked to its **source (sensor, simulation model, processing component)** via the DSRM, enabling **root-cause analysis** of deviations.
  - **Validation Mechanisms**:
    - **Deviation analysis**: Simulated data (ˆr_out) is compared to **measured output (r_out)** to quantify friction-induced errors.
    - **Dynamic model updates**: The DS supports **continuous refinement** of friction models during operation to account for **wear, temperature, or load changes**.
  - **Uncertainty Sources**:
    - **Model inaccuracies**: Analytical friction models may not capture all dynamic effects (e.g., Stribeck effect), necessitating **data-driven (black-box) model supplementation**.
    - **Sensor noise**: Encoder measurements may introduce **positional uncertainty**, mitigated via **dual-encoder calibration**."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital twin (DS) is established via:
  - **Network Protocol**: **MQTT** for **real-time data streaming** of sensor data (e.g., I_M, r_out) from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller to the DS.
  - **Data Format**: Time-series data is transmitted as **floating-point arrays** of torque (τ) and angular velocity (ω) for each data trace (e.g., r_M, r_G).
  - **Synchronization**: The DS **subscribes to MQTT topics** corresponding to sensor channels, ensuring **temporal alignment** between PT measurements and DS processing.
  - **Architecture**: The DS operates as a **standalone processing unit** (e.g., local server or cloud-based) connected to the PT via **industrial Ethernet** (implied by SINUMERIK integration)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital twin is hosted and deployed as follows:
  - **Execution Environment**: The DS’s **Simulation components** (Simulink models) and **Processing logic** are executed on a **local or cloud-based server** (not specified), enabling real-time processing of MQTT-streamed data.
  - **Deployment Architecture**:
    - **Centralized**: The DS is implemented as a **single, unified system** for the first axis, with no mention of distributed deployment.
    - **Integration**: The DS is **tightly coupled** with the PT’s CNC controller (SINUMERIK 840D sl) via MQTT, ensuring **low-latency data flow**.
  - **Scalability**: The current implementation is **axis-specific** (first axis only), but the methodology allows for **modular expansion** to other axes via the DSRM’s **concrete realization** framework."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin system adheres to the following standards and methodologies:
  - **Modeling Standards**:
    - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**: Used for **structural and behavioral modeling** of the first axis (compliant with OMG SysML v1.7).
    - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: A **domain-specific reference model** (adapted from [15]) for defining DS components (e.g., Purpose, DataTraces, Processing).
  - **Simulation Standards**:
    - **MathWorks Simulink**: Industry-standard tool for **executable simulation models** (e.g., friction dynamics).
  - **Data Protocols**:
    - **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)**: Industry-standard protocol for **real-time sensor data streaming** in industrial IoT applications.
  - **Industrial Automation**:
    - **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl**: Standard CNC controller for **industrial robots**, ensuring compatibility with the PT’s data interfaces."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The digital twin system includes the following security and safety considerations:
  - **Passive Data Flow**: The DS operates as a **digital shadow**, adhering to the **one-way data flow** principle (no autonomous control or feedback to the PT), thus **eliminating risks of unintended physical actuation**.
  - **Data Isolation**: MQTT streams are **not described as exposed to external threats**, but standard industrial practices (e.g., firewalls, VPNs) are implied for **network security**.
  - **Safety Constraints**:
    - The robot’s **safety cell** restricts the working range to [-90°, 90°], ensuring **physical safety** during operation.
    - The DS does not **override PT control commands**, mitigating risks of **accidental damage** or **human injury**.
  - **Operational Safety**: The DS’s **visualization dashboard** enables **operator monitoring** of friction-induced deviations, supporting **predictive maintenance** and **safe operation**."
]
